title: Lap-Group Ranker With Raw Categoricals
summary: Pit-now is partly a relative decision: on a given lap, some cars are much more stop-ready than others even when global hazard varies by race.
rationale: Pit-now is partly a relative decision: on a given lap, some cars are much more stop-ready than others even when global hazard varies by race. Ranking objectives are built for within-group ordering, and CatBoost/LightGBM both support ranking while handling high-cardinality structure better than manual one-hot pipelines.
implementation_hint: Create groups by `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` and train a ranking specialist (`CatBoost YetiRankPairwise/QueryAUC` or `LightGBM lambdarank`) using raw `Driver/Race/Compound` plus the best numeric features. Use its OOF score as a blend feature for the pointwise AUC model rather than replacing the classifier outright.
expected_effect: Better within-lap ordering and improved robustness when absolute stop propensity shifts strongly across tracks.
risk: A pure ranker may miscalibrate probabilities and can underperform on singleton or tiny groups, so blend and validate race-held-out only.
